content_signals.body_excerpt:
co-authored with claude

Overview:
- Introduces `bin/snappy` and `infra/snappy`. The bin will be ran as a sidecar against `base-node-reth` and `base-consensus` and will pause the `base-node-reth` container, run `Reth::SnapshotManifestCommand` to produce a manifest.json and all the tar files, and unpause `base-node-reth`
- Utilizes the `bollard` package to manage the pause/unpause state of docker containers
- Utilizes the S3 SDK as R2/MinIO are all S3-API compatible 

Tests:
- Wrote integration tests (pause, unpause, uploading to MinIO)
- Wrote an e2e test (pause -> upload -> unpause)

Up next:
- [ ] Wiring up the `reth download` command to `crates/execution`
- [ ] Optimizations to upload only diffs. That is, not uploading committed static files anymore
commit_subjects: spike
use SnapshotCommand wiring + similar manifest structure + add e2e
rename + err handling + parallelize + creds + nits + multi-part upload
perf: snapshotter to only upload diffs for static files (#2583)
perf: skip compression on finalized static files (#2700)
feedback

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Delete the remaining engine priority queue, task enum, trait dispatch, enqueue, drain, and delegated/finalize wrapper structs. The engine processor now publishes direct-operation state changes without a pre-drain step, and Engine is a non-generic state owner with generic methods for the client-specific calls. The legacy dev queue-length RPC now reports zero without carrying queue state through the engine actor.
commit_subjects: refactor(consensus): delete engine task queue
fix(consensus): satisfy engine clippy bounds
Simplify consensus engine operations

content_signals.body_excerpt: Adds V2 proof storage tables, history-aware cursors, and current-state persistence so proof reads can reconstruct historical state from old-value changesets. The MDBX backend now seeds initial state into V2 current tables and keeps V2 history in sync across writes, prune, unwind, and replacement flows.
commit_subjects: feat(proofs): add v2 proof storage schema
feat(proofs): add v2 proof storage cursors
feat(proofs): persist v2 proof storage state
feat(proofs): make v2 proof state authoritative
feat(proofs): use v2 cursors for proof batches
fix(proofs): satisfy v2 cursor clippy
fix(proofs): clean proof provider test clippy
fix(proofs): clean live proof test clippy
fix(proofs): support metrics storage test helpers
fix(proofs): harden v2 key decoding
fix(proofs): repair v2 trie cursor misses
fix(proofs): clean v2 clippy warnings
fix(proofs): address v2 cursor review notes
fix(proofs): remove stale cursor imports
fix(proofs): address v2 review edge cases
fix(proofs): clarify v2 cursor history semantics
fix(proofs): borrow v2 trie subkey conversion

content_signals.body_excerpt: ## Summary This schedules the Zeronet Beryl activation for June 1, 2026 at 1:00pm EDT. It updates the built-in chain config, keeps the embedded Zeronet genesis Base fork stanza aligned, and adjusts the focused activation assertions for the new timestamp. Timestamp verified with https://www.unixtimestamp.com/
commit_subjects: feat(chains): schedule zeronet beryl activation

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Auto-generated PR to sync Cargo.toml version with release branch.

- **Release branch**: `releases/v0.10.0`
- **Target version**: `0.10.0`

Once merged, run the **Create Release** workflow to build a release candidate.
commit_subjects: chore(release): set version to 0.10.0

key_prs.author: haardikk21

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

PR4 of the EIP-8130 (account abstraction) rollout. Flips the devp2p transaction-pool validator from PR3's hard-reject of type-byte 0x7D to a **conservative accept** path that runs every spec-mandated structural check while still keeping all execution-time semantics (verifier dispatch, state lookups, fork gating) deferred to later PRs.

RPC ingress (ingress-rpc + execution-rpc) remains closed; local-origin submissions from devp2p are also rejected. Hardfork gating lands in PR6 against **Cobalt** (the upgrade after Beryl; `BaseUpgrade::Cobalt` doesn't exist in this repo yet and will be added by PR6).

## What changed

**Sender recovery (commit 1)** — fixes a hidden break in PR3: `BasePooledTransaction::SignerRecoverable` for the EIP-8130 variant was previously returning `tx.explicit_sender()` unconditionally, meaning EOA-path transactions (`sender = None`) could never be indexed in the pool. Adds `Eip8130Signed::recover_eoa_sender()` which:

- returns `Ok(None)` for configured-owner txs (sender already explicit)
- recovers from the 65-byte `sender_auth` against `sender_signature_hash()` for EOA txs
- returns `Err` on malformed payloads

**Mempool policy constants (comm...
commit_subjects: feat(consensus): recover EOA sender for EIP-8130 pool indexing
feat(consensus): add EIP-8130 mempool policy constants
feat(txpool): accept EIP-8130 with structural validation
test(txpool): EIP-8130 structural validation
fix(txpool): EIP-8130 already-expired and ConfigChange owner_changes
fix(consensus): honor unchecked EIP-8130 sender recovery contract
fix(consensus): EIP-8130 envelope honors EOA recovery + checked/unchecked split
fix(txpool): EIP-8130 cap owner entries + revoke-check on config auth
fix(txpool,consensus): EIP-8130 unify auth verifier range + dedupe recovery

key_prs.title: feat(txpool): EIP-8130 conservative-accept gate (PR4 of N)

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Refreshes `crates/proof/succinct/elf/manifest.toml` from a fresh SP1 ELF build after a merge to trunk (`main`).

Generated by https://github.com/base/base/actions/runs/26483962587.

## Validation

- `just succinct build-elfs`
- `just succinct write-manifest`
- `python3 etc/scripts/local/check-elf-manifest.py --print-hashes crates/proof/succinct/elf/manifest.toml crates/proof/succinct/elf`

## Hashes

```text
range-elf-embedded: expected="45cb78e7f82403dd10e67301e22e3e128ec8df511b7d6a22a3eb33b464afd0a7" actual="45cb78e7f82403dd10e67301e22e3e128ec8df511b7d6a22a3eb33b464afd0a7"
aggregation-elf: expected="baf0a889a5c493d780235b14f21e4f6c94feb8d4d8e138224f0d7d8572c944b9" actual="baf0a889a5c493d780235b14f21e4f6c94feb8d4d8e138224f0d7d8572c944b9"
```

## Diff

```diff
diff --git a/crates/proof/succinct/elf/manifest.toml b/crates/proof/succinct/elf/manifest.toml
index 88a50a0..adc9af2 100644
--- a/crates/proof/succinct/elf/manifest.toml
+++ b/crates/proof/succinct/elf/manifest.toml
@@ -17,7 +17,7 @@
 
 [[elfs]]
 name = "range-elf-embedded"
-sha256 = "fc20d749763f788b9c54cac58399d09eae1396d3e187cc0c22240b941f4ad0ed"
+sha256 = "45cb78e7f82403dd10e67301e22e3e128ec8df511b7d6a22a3...
commit_subjects: fix(proof): refresh succinct elf manifest

key_prs.author: sethoshi18

content_signals.body_excerpt:
This commit resolves three high-value items identified during a repository audit:

1. Websocket Proxy Filter Refactor: Replaced brittle `unwrap()` pattern during string parsing in `crates/infra/websocket-proxy/src/filter.rs` with safe `let Ok(value)` destructuring to improve stability and avoid potential panics.

2. S3 Audit Log Event Support: Extended `archive_event` in `crates/infra/audit/src/storage.rs` to support handling all `BundleEvent` variants instead of explicitly returning an error. Fallback uses either the bundle hash (if present) or the bundle ID.

3. S3 Audit Log Exponential Backoff: Implemented exponential backoff using the `backon` crate to retry `put_object` requests when uploading events to S3. This resolves a known issue (TODO) where transient network failures would drop audit logs.
commit_subjects: fix: improve reliability of websocket proxy and s3 audit logs

key_prs.title: fix: improve reliability of websocket proxy and s3 audit logs

key_prs.author: Alicepoltora

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

`decode_eip_1559_params()` returns `(elasticity, denominator)` (documented in `HoloceneExtraData::decode_params`). The pattern:

```rust
Some((0, e)) if e != 0 => {
    error!("Holocene EIP1559 params cannot have a 0 denominator unless elasticity is also 0...");
```

fires when **elasticity = 0** and denominator ≠ 0, but the error message says **"0 denominator"** — the two fields are swapped.

## Root cause

The tuple returned by `decode_eip_1559_params()` is `(elasticity_multiplier, max_change_denominator)`. The destructuring variable names `ae` / `ad` (`a`ttribute `e`lasticity / `a`ttribute `d`enominator) confirm the intent, but the prose in the error message accidentally describes the opposite field.

## Changes

1. **Fix the error message** for the zero-elasticity arm: `"0 denominator"` → `"0 elasticity multiplier"`.
2. **Add the missing guard** for the symmetric case `Some((ae, 0)) if ae != 0` — non-zero elasticity with a zero denominator was silently accepted and would cause a division-by-zero in downstream base fee calculations.
3. **Update `AttributesMismatch::InvalidEIP1559ParamsCombination`** doc comment to cover both invalid cases.
4. **Fix the misleading ...
commit_subjects: fix(engine): correct swapped EIP-1559 error message and add missing zero-denominator check

key_prs.title: fix(engine): correct swapped EIP-1559 error message and add missing zero-denominator guard

key_prs.author: Danielkis97

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Fixes a panic in `StateProcessor::build_pending_state` when called with empty flashblock input.

The previous code called `.unwrap()` on the minimum key of an empty `BTreeMap`, which could panic. This now returns `Ok(None)` when there is no pending state to build.

Fixes #2785

## Testing

- `cargo +nightly fmt`

I attempted `cargo check -p base-flashblocks` locally on Windows, but the build is blocked by a native `librocksdb-sys`/MSVC toolchain error unrelated to this change.
commit_subjects: fix(flashblocks): handle empty pending state input

key_prs.title: fix(flashblocks): handle empty pending state input

content_signals.body_excerpt:
This commit resolves three high-value items identified during a repository audit:

1. Websocket Proxy Filter Refactor: Replaced brittle `unwrap()` pattern during string parsing in `crates/infra/websocket-proxy/src/filter.rs` with safe `let Ok(value)` destructuring to improve stability and avoid potential panics.

2. S3 Audit Log Event Support: Extended `archive_event` in `crates/infra/audit/src/storage.rs` to support handling all `BundleEvent` variants instead of explicitly returning an error. Fallback uses either the bundle hash (if present) or the bundle ID.

3. S3 Audit Log Exponential Backoff: Implemented exponential backoff using the `backon` crate to retry `put_object` requests when uploading events to S3. This resolves a known issue (TODO) where transient network failures would drop audit logs.
commit_subjects: fix: improve reliability of websocket proxy and s3 audit logs

closed_unmerged.title: fix: improve reliability of websocket proxy and s3 audit logs

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Bug

In `find_starting_forkchoice()`, the safe block search cursor was initialized with `current_fc.un_safe` (the unsafe/unconfirmed head) instead of `current_fc.safe` (the known-safe head).

```rust
// Before (buggy)
let mut safe_cursor = current_fc.un_safe;

// After (fixed)
let mut safe_cursor = current_fc.safe;
```

## Impact

On every node restart, the algorithm unnecessarily traverses every block between the unsafe head and the actual safe head before finding the correct safe block. In practice, the unsafe head can be hundreds or thousands of blocks ahead of the safe head (the sequencing window on Base mainnet is 3600 L1 blocks), making sync start significantly slower than it needs to be.

## Why the fix is safe

The loop already handles the general case correctly: it checks `is_behind_sequence_window || is_finalized || is_genesis` and walks backwards if needed. Starting from `current_fc.safe` means:
- If the existing safe block already satisfies the sequencing window condition (the common case), the loop exits immediately.
- If for some reason the safe block needs to be pushed further back, the loop continues walking backwards exactly as before.

Correctness is unchang...
commit_subjects: fix(sync): initialize safe_cursor from safe FC instead of unsafe

closed_unmerged.title: fix(sync): initialize safe_cursor from safe FC instead of unsafe

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Delete the remaining engine priority queue, task enum, trait dispatch, enqueue, drain, and delegated/finalize wrapper structs. The engine processor now publishes direct-operation state changes without a pre-drain step, and Engine is a non-generic state owner with generic methods for the client-specific calls. The legacy dev queue-length RPC now reports zero without carrying queue state through the engine actor.
commit_subjects: refactor(consensus): delete engine task queue
fix(consensus): satisfy engine clippy bounds
Simplify consensus engine operations

Generated by https://github.com/base/base/actions/runs/26483962587.

content_signals.body_excerpt: Adds V2 proof storage tables, history-aware cursors, and current-state persistence so proof reads can reconstruct historical state from old-value changesets. The MDBX backend now seeds initial state into V2 current tables and keeps V2 history in sync across writes, prune, unwind, and replacement flows.

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

PR4 of the EIP-8130 (account abstraction) rollout. Flips the devp2p transaction-pool validator from PR3's hard-reject of type-byte 0x7D to a **conservative accept** path that runs every spec-mandated structural check while still keeping all execution-time semantics (verifier dispatch, state lookups, fork gating) deferred to later PRs.

RPC ingress (ingress-rpc + execution-rpc) remains closed; local-origin submissions from devp2p are also rejected. Hardfork gating lands in PR6 against **Cobalt** (the upgrade after Beryl; `BaseUpgrade::Cobalt` doesn't exist in this repo yet and will be added by PR6).

## What changed

**Sender recovery (commit 1)** — fixes a hidden break in PR3: `BasePooledTransaction::SignerRecoverable` for the EIP-8130 variant was previously returning `tx.explicit_sender()` unconditionally, meaning EOA-path transactions (`sender = None`) could never be indexed in the pool. Adds `Eip8130Signed::recover_eoa_sender()` which:

- returns `Ok(None)` for configured-owner txs (sender already explicit)
- recovers from the 65-byte `sender_auth` against `sender_signature_hash()` for EOA txs
- returns `Err` on malformed payloads

**Mempool policy constants (comm...
commit_subjects: feat(consensus): recover EOA sender for EIP-8130 pool indexing
feat(consensus): add EIP-8130 mempool policy constants
feat(txpool): accept EIP-8130 with structural validation
test(txpool): EIP-8130 structural validation
fix(txpool): EIP-8130 already-expired and ConfigChange owner_changes
fix(consensus): honor unchecked EIP-8130 sender recovery contract
fix(consensus): EIP-8130 envelope honors EOA recovery + checked/unchecked split
fix(txpool): EIP-8130 cap owner entries + revoke-check on config auth
fix(txpool,consensus): EIP-8130 unify auth verifier range + dedupe recovery

opened.title: feat(txpool): EIP-8130 conservative-accept gate (PR4 of N)

content_signals.body_excerpt: ## Summary This schedules the Zeronet Beryl activation for June 1, 2026 at 1:00pm EDT. It updates the built-in chain config, keeps the embedded Zeronet genesis Base fork stanza aligned, and adjusts the focused activation assertions for the new timestamp. Timestamp verified with https://www.unixtimestamp.com/
commit_subjects: feat(chains): schedule zeronet beryl activation

content_signals.body_excerpt:
This commit resolves three high-value items identified during a repository audit:

1. Websocket Proxy Filter Refactor: Replaced brittle `unwrap()` pattern during string parsing in `crates/infra/websocket-proxy/src/filter.rs` with safe `let Ok(value)` destructuring to improve stability and avoid potential panics.

2. S3 Audit Log Event Support: Extended `archive_event` in `crates/infra/audit/src/storage.rs` to support handling all `BundleEvent` variants instead of explicitly returning an error. Fallback uses either the bundle hash (if present) or the bundle ID.

3. S3 Audit Log Exponential Backoff: Implemented exponential backoff using the `backon` crate to retry `put_object` requests when uploading events to S3. This resolves a known issue (TODO) where transient network failures would drop audit logs.
commit_subjects: fix: improve reliability of websocket proxy and s3 audit logs

opened.title: fix: improve reliability of websocket proxy and s3 audit logs

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

`decode_eip_1559_params()` returns `(elasticity, denominator)` (documented in `HoloceneExtraData::decode_params`). The pattern:

```rust
Some((0, e)) if e != 0 => {
    error!("Holocene EIP1559 params cannot have a 0 denominator unless elasticity is also 0...");
```

fires when **elasticity = 0** and denominator ≠ 0, but the error message says **"0 denominator"** — the two fields are swapped.

## Root cause

The tuple returned by `decode_eip_1559_params()` is `(elasticity_multiplier, max_change_denominator)`. The destructuring variable names `ae` / `ad` (`a`ttribute `e`lasticity / `a`ttribute `d`enominator) confirm the intent, but the prose in the error message accidentally describes the opposite field.

## Changes

1. **Fix the error message** for the zero-elasticity arm: `"0 denominator"` → `"0 elasticity multiplier"`.
2. **Add the missing guard** for the symmetric case `Some((ae, 0)) if ae != 0` — non-zero elasticity with a zero denominator was silently accepted and would cause a division-by-zero in downstream base fee calculations.
3. **Update `AttributesMismatch::InvalidEIP1559ParamsCombination`** doc comment to cover both invalid cases.
4. **Fix the misleading ...
commit_subjects: fix(engine): correct swapped EIP-1559 error message and add missing zero-denominator check

opened.title: fix(engine): correct swapped EIP-1559 error message and add missing zero-denominator guard

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Bug

In `find_starting_forkchoice()`, the safe block search cursor was initialized with `current_fc.un_safe` (the unsafe/unconfirmed head) instead of `current_fc.safe` (the known-safe head).

```rust
// Before (buggy)
let mut safe_cursor = current_fc.un_safe;

// After (fixed)
let mut safe_cursor = current_fc.safe;
```

## Impact

On every node restart, the algorithm unnecessarily traverses every block between the unsafe head and the actual safe head before finding the correct safe block. In practice, the unsafe head can be hundreds or thousands of blocks ahead of the safe head (the sequencing window on Base mainnet is 3600 L1 blocks), making sync start significantly slower than it needs to be.

## Why the fix is safe

The loop already handles the general case correctly: it checks `is_behind_sequence_window || is_finalized || is_genesis` and walks backwards if needed. Starting from `current_fc.safe` means:
- If the existing safe block already satisfies the sequencing window condition (the common case), the loop exits immediately.
- If for some reason the safe block needs to be pushed further back, the loop continues walking backwards exactly as before.

Correctness is unchang...
commit_subjects: fix(sync): initialize safe_cursor from safe FC instead of unsafe

opened.title: fix(sync): initialize safe_cursor from safe FC instead of unsafe

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Fixes a panic in `StateProcessor::build_pending_state` when called with empty flashblock input.

The previous code called `.unwrap()` on the minimum key of an empty `BTreeMap`, which could panic. This now returns `Ok(None)` when there is no pending state to build.

Fixes #2785

## Testing

- `cargo +nightly fmt`

I attempted `cargo check -p base-flashblocks` locally on Windows, but the build is blocked by a native `librocksdb-sys`/MSVC toolchain error unrelated to this change.
commit_subjects: fix(flashblocks): handle empty pending state input

opened.title: fix(flashblocks): handle empty pending state input
